name: Strawberry Chiffon Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare CHOCOLATE PASTRY.
2. In a medium bowl, dissolve gelatin in boiling water; cool slightly. Crush strawberries or puree to equal 3/4 cup. Stir strawberry puree into the gelatin mixture; refrigerate until partially set (consistency of beaten egg whites). Fold whipped topping into the strawberry mixture and spoon into prepared crust. Refrigerate 2 to 3 hours or until set.
3. Line a baking tray with wax paper. In a small, microwave-safe bowl, place chocolate chips and shortening. Microwave at HIGH for 1 minute; stir. If necessary, heat additional 15 seconds at a time, stirring after each heating, just until chips are melted when stirred. Dip whole strawberries into the melted chocolate and place on prepared tray. Refrigerate, uncovered, about 30 minutes or until chocolate is firm. Just before serving, garnish pie with chocolate-covered strawberries. Cover; refrigerate leftovers.
CHOCOLATE PASTRY:
1. In a medium bowl, stir together flour, sugar, cocoa, and salt.
2. In a measuring cup, place oil and add water. Do not stir. Pour liquid over the flour mixture; stir lightly with a fork until well blended (if the mixture is too dry, add 1 to 2 teaspoons of additional cold water).
3. With hands, shape the mixture into a ball. Place between two pieces of wax paper and roll into a 12-inch circle. Peel off the top sheet of paper.
4. Gently invert the pastry over a 9-inch pie plate; peel off the paper. Fit the pastry into the pie plate. Fold under extra pastry around the edge and flute the edge. Prick the bottom and sides of the crust thoroughly with a fork. Refrigerate about 30 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, preheat the oven to 450°F (230°C). Bake the crust for 10 minutes; cool completely.
